feed.title: Erik's Ponderings
feed.subtitle: Erik's blog, essays, and other content.

entry.title: Restoring a TeleVideo TS-800A Terminal
entry.summary: Introduction Concurrently with my work on the 1979 TeleVideo TVI-912, I acquired a TeleVideo TS-800A. The terminal in its glory The full name of the device is “TeleVideo TS 800A Satellite User Station”. In the early 1980s, TeleVideo started building CP/M computers. Some were standalone, but the 800A has the particularity of having no local drives: neither hard drives, nor floppy drive...

entry.title: The TeleVideo TVI-912 terminal - Part 3
entry.summary: Background See also: The TeleVideo TVI-912 terminal - Part 1, where I gather background information about the TeleVideo TVI-912 terminal, as well as my initial cleaning and testing of the terminal The TeleVideo TVI-912 terminal - Part 2, where I fix the video control board and memory and manage to ge the terminal working RAM IC sockets The next thing I wanted to do was to replace the ...

entry.title: The TeleVideo TVI-912 terminal - Part 2
entry.summary: Background In my previous article, I gathered some background information about the TeleVideo TVI-912 terminal, and I covered the initial cleaning and testing of the terminal. The bottom line: although it did power on and beeped, showing that there was good life in the main control board, and the CRT also showed some signs of life, the terminal didn’t display anything. Video control board I ...

entry.title: The TeleVideo TVI-912 terminal - Part 1
entry.summary: Introduction I recently won a computer terminal at a local auction. I bid on it because I had been hoping to find a vintage computer terminal for a while, so I could connect it to my Altair-Duino or PiDP-11 kits. This one looked cool, I thought I would not win the auction, and then I did!1 See also The TeleVideo TVI-912 terminal - Part 2. The terminal as I got it Esthetically, I find...

entry.title: Yamaha Clavinova CLP-280 felt and rubber contacts replacement

link.title: Yamaha Clavinova CLP-280 felt and rubber contacts replacement

entry.summary: The symptoms Recently, I started feeling that the touch of our 15-year-old Yamaha Clavinova CLP-280 in the mid-range was different: the responsiveness of the keys was not good anymore, and different from the lower and higher ranges. In addition, a couple of keys occasionally made a clacking sound. The Yamaha Clavinova CLP-280 Given what I know about the CLP-280, I figured that there m...
